res.text: File formats to analyse with OCR:
Do not OCR PDFs with existing searchable text
Reduced functionality version, some features will be hidden or disabled. Free for both personal and commercial use.
If you use this product in a commercial environment you are now required to register it, but don't worry it doesn't cost anything to register.
For a FREE registration code please visit the Mythicsoft web site:
If you use the product only for personal use then you are kindly requested, but not required, to register the product.
Commercial use, I would like to enter my registration code
Note: Drag + Drop to re-order fields
&Enter each independent search term on a separate line
Reference an existing index or group
Do you want to create a new index, new group, or reference an existing index?
Specify the path of the index store you wish to add:
Do you want the index to automatically update?
Include all other files using name only
Note: You can combine terms using standard Boolean operators such as AND, OR, and NOT.
Limit memory consumption on large searches
Only apply truncation to search history
Note: Drag + Drop to re-order fields
(Add each valid file type on a separate line.)
&Binary data is not appropriate for searching
Press the button below to restore extension settings to the installation defaults.
This configuration group controls the extension settings for advanced file processing.
This configuration group contains general settings used to control searches.
This configuration group controls which editor will be used when working with the Contents View.
This configuration group contains the advanced settings.
Which history you would like cleared?
Default following file types to UTF-8 if no specific encoding information detected.
(Add each valid file type on a separate line.)
Include IFilters for HTML/XML based file types
- Currently selected file in long path format
Limit max characters displayed per line to:
Other valid End of Line (EOL) identifiers
Include folder path in file name search
Warning: Setting the thread count too high can affect performance.
Add to a &Folder's context menu options
Add to a F&ile's context menu options
Add to Find/&Search Start menu options
* UAC Note: Changing Shell settings may cause the Shell admin EXE to launch with privilege escalation required.
Basic context menu options for complex selections
Include parent message name in attachment name
Add separate message body text attachment
Remove HTML markup from message body
Select the XSLT processor used for Save Results custom format transforms:
Select which criteria fields accept file lists (e.g., =c:\list.txt):
Process stylized quotes as normal quotes
A Search Filter can be any valid value for the Look In or File name field. When active it is automatically applied to every search.
Select the search method for searching common Office document formats (e.g. doc, pdf, xls, etc):
Deep Search (searches both the Text and the raw data in the file)
Also search raw document data of processed files
Select which compressed/archive formats you wish to search within:
Search within only selected compressed/archive formats:
This configuration group contains the expression settings.
* Takes effect for all newly loaded files.
Copyright © 2026 Mythicsoft Ltd. All rights reserved.
Privacy policy: Personal information will NOT be sold or distributed

Not quite sure if your regular expression is correct? This window allows you to test the regular expression engine. Simply enter the expression you wish to test along with some sample text and then press Test.
Note: The expression tester uses Perl style regular expressions.
What is the name of the file you want to find?
You don't know the exact name? Not to worry, you can search on parts of the name. If you don't know any part of the name leave it blank.
Would find all documents having My and then Doc somewhere else in the name
Make sure the file name is set to Wildcard expression in the options tab before using expressions like those shown in the examples.
Would find all documents with MyDoc somwhere in the name
&Start the search automatically when you click the Finish button
Where do you want to search for the file?
Do you want to search the folder's sub folders as well?
Performance tip: Choosing a specific folder greatly reduces the number of files to be searched and so is usually much faster.
Do you want to search for the file based upon text within the file?
&Yes, search the contents of the file
What text should the file contain?
Would find any file that had a line with the phrase Dear Bill in it.
(e.g. My Dear Bill..., Dear Billy how are you... etc.)
Would find any file that had both the words Dear and Bill in it (e.g. Dear Fred how is Bill..., The dearest items should be rebilled...)
Performance tip: Searching the contents of files can take a long time, especially if a name has not been entered or is not very specific. Therefore, choosing No is usually much faster.
Note: The name and email address must be entered exactly as shown in the registration email.
If you have any problems please contact support@mythicsoft.com for assistance.
Note: Drag && Drop to rearrange items
Welcome to the Expression Wizard. This wizard will help you define exactly what you are looking for.
Regular expressions are used to describe patterns for string matching. While regular expressions are very powerful they also tend to be daunting for the uninitiated.

Expression Wizard's purpose is therefore to reduce the complexity of regular expressions without sacrificing the power. Expression Wizard will help build the expression for you by asking you to describe what you are looking in terms easier to understand.
The search specific note goes here.
&Yes. Please insert appropriate backslashes because none of the characters I entered should be treated as special characters.
&No. I am deliberately using special characters for advanced searching.
One or more of the expressions you entered contains at least one of the following special characters: ^$*?+.[]()|\

Special characters are used in regular expressions to define the pattern matching rules and therefore should be used with caution. If you want to search for a character that is also a special character that you must put a backslash before the character.
Would you like Expression Wizard to automatically insert the backslashes before all special characters?
Wildcard expressions use the standard Windows syntax for specifying a group of files. A semi-colon is used to separate multiple expressions.
*.txt\t\tspecifies all files with a .txt extension\n\n*.xls;*.doc\tspecifies all files with either a .xls or a .doc extension

*.png;*.jpg:holiday		specifies all .png and .jpg files with the word holiday in their name

NOT:*.doc;*.xls		specifies all files except .doc and .xls files

holiday;NOT:*.exe;*.dll	specifies all files except .exe or .dll files with the word holiday in their name
Boolean expressions use the web style search operators AND, OR, NOT, LIKE and NEAR. Boolean searches can be either by line or by file:
work AND document\tsearches for the words 'work' and 'document' in the same line/file
work document\t\tperforms the same search as above (the AND is implicit)
work OR document\tsearches for the words 'work' OR 'document' in the same line/file
work NEAR document\tsearches for 'work' close to the word 'document' in a line/file
"work document"\tsearches for the exact phrase 'work document'
expression is separately tested against each individual line
expression is tested across the whole file
Note: Use parentheses to group expressions, e.g. (work AND doc) OR (special AND case)
Boolean expressions use the web style search operators AND, OR, and NOT. Boolean searches are matched across the whole file.
work AND document\tsearches for the words 'work' and 'document' in the same file
work document\t\tperforms the same search as above (the AND is implicit)
work OR document\tsearches for the words 'work' OR 'document' in the same file
work NOT document\tsearches for 'work' without the word 'document' in the same file
"work document"\tsearches for the exact phrase 'work document'
Note: Use parentheses to group expressions, e.g. (work AND doc) OR (special AND case)
Plain Text treats the search criteria text entered as a literal value, i.e. exactly as the text is entered in the edit field. The search criteria matches against all or part of a word.
the author\twould find phrases like 'the author' or 'the authors'.
File Hashing generates a fixed length unique identifier for each file based on the contents of the file. Files with identical contents will produce identical hash values.
1. Specify the hash algorithms in the Configuration settings
2. Enter the hash value to search for as either a comma separated list of hash values or a pointer to a list of hash values, e.g.
Boolean RegEx expressions are a hybrid of Boolean and Regular Expressions. Regular expressions can be combined using the familiar boolean operators AND, OR, and NOT.
Note: Use parentheses to group expressions, e.g. (work AND doc) OR (special AND case)

Whole Word expressions are a similar to Boolean expressions except that each term only matches to exact words in the search text, i.e. excludes partial word matches. Terms can be combined using the familiar boolean operators AND, OR, and NOT
the author\tsearches for the specific words 'the' and 'author' excluding partial matches such as 'these' or 'authority'.

Fuzzy search expressions match the approximate search terms, in exactly the same way as the LIKE operator for Boolean Expressions. Since Fuzzy search expressions are a special type of Boolean expression individual search phrases can be combined using the AND, OR, and NOT operators.
separate\tmatches both separate and seperate but also separated and separately.
collected\tmatches collected as well as collects and connected.
Multi-line regex matches regular expressions over multiple lines.
matches the word thought along with up-to 20 characters either side including characters on leading/trailing lines.
Select the file formats you want to search within:
Add this search as a favorite.
Add each folder on a separate line by either typing directly, adding from drop down, or browsing.
* Changes take effect after you restart
Add a new or existing index\nAdd Index
Remove or permanently delete index\nRemove or Delete Index
Update the index with any changes that may have occurred\nUpdate Index
Edit the indexes associated with the schedule\nEdit Index List
One or more of the selected files are composite files and will not be deleted. Do you want to continue?

Registration key has expired. Please purchase a new key or install an older version of the product. Older product versions are available in the MyAccount section of the Mythicsoft web site (www.mythicsoft.com).
Switching to a previous search will stop the current search. Do you want to continue?
Create a new search based on last search\nNew search based on last search
Save the current search criteria\nSave Criteria
Save the current search criteria with a new name\nSave As
Change the printer and printing options\nPrint Setup
Display program information, version number and copyright\nAbout
Switch to the next window pane\nNext Pane
Switch back to the previous window pane\nPrevious Pane
Open another window for the active document\nNew Window
Arrange icons at the bottom of the window\nArrange Icons
Arrange windows so they overlap\nCascade Windows
Arrange windows as non-overlapping tiles\nTile Windows
Split the active window into panes\nSplit
Copy the current selection to the clipboard\nCopy
Cut the selection and put it on the Clipboard\nCut
Replace specific text with different text\nReplace
Show or hide the toolbar\nToggle Toolbar
Show or hide the status bar\nToggle Status Bar
Reduce the window to an icon
Enlarge the window to full size
Switch to the next document window
Switch to the previous document window
Close the active window and prompts to save the documents
Restore the window to normal size

This pane displays any files selected in the File List view. If you are searching by Contents then this pane also displays lines found within the selected files. For available options use the right mouse button to bring up the context menu.
%s is not a valid search directory. The directory name must be a valid path, including the drive letter (e.g. C:\Windows) OR a properly constructed UNC name (e.g. \\WORKSTATION\Shared_Folder).
Copy the paths of the currently selected file(s) to the clipboard\nCopy file paths
Open properties dialog for currently selected file(s)\nOpen properties
Open the currently selected file\nOpen file
Switch word wrapping on/off when viewing contents\nWord Wrap
Set the font for the contents view\nSet Font
Select all the files in the File List view\nSelect All
Clear history items in the drop down lists\nClear History
System ran out of memory during search. Try the search again with the 'One Phase Search' option. If the problem persists please contact technical support.
System encountered an unexpected exception during the search. If the problem persists please contact technical support.
Please re-search to see the effects of the new settings.
Open an Explorer instance at the path for the currently selected file(s)\nOpen Explorer
Goes to the previous search results\nPrevious search result
Goes to the next search results\nNext search result
Goes to the previous search results\nPrevious search result
Goes to the next search results\nNext search result
More information on the chosen expression type
Specifies whether or not the content is matched on a line by line basis or across the whole file
Specifies if file name expression is a NOT expression
Show or hide the contents view pane\nShow/Hide Contents View
Create new search with default settings\nNew blank search
Create new search based on current criteria\nNew search with current criteria
Scroll to the next highlighted line of found text\nScroll to next
Scroll to the previous highlighted line of found text\nScroll to previous
Toggle the highlighting of the current find\nToggle highlighting
Edit the currently selected favorite\nEdit Favorite
Delete the currently selected favorite\nDelete Favorite
Return to the main view of the selection\nReturn to main view
Add current search as new favorite\nAdd Favorite
You must set the file name to 'Regular Expression' in the Options tab before you can specify the File name as a regular expression.
View the contents found by selecting one or more files in the right hand pane.\n\nNote: To print this pane click Print on the File menu.
You must set the Contents to 'Regular Expression' in the Options tab before you can specify the Containing text as a regular expression.

Expression wizard does not provide all the functionality of regular expressions but if you know how to construct regular expressions you can specify any regular expression yourself. Just add your regular expression as type 'Phrase' in the 'Followed by' list and switch off escapes on the next wizard page (which is enabled once you start entering special characters).
Set the colour for highlighting found text\nSet Colour
Save the contents view data\nSave Contents View
Toggle One Phase Searching for efficient contents searching of large drives (no progress meter)\nToggle One Phase Searching (for large drives)
Dock the Contents View below the File List View\nDock below
Dock the Contents View to the right of the File List View\nDock to right
Show help dialog for command line parameters\nCommand line parms
More information on the chosen expression type
Search within the current result list\nSearch within current result list
Show/Hide contents view pane\nToggle Contents View
Search within the currently selected files\nSearch within current selection
Toggle the highlighting of the original search\nToggle highlighting
Open the folder where all the favorites are saved\nOpen Favorites Folder
Close all Windows/Tabs except the active search\nClose all but active search
Close all open file windows\nClose all files
Save the current search session\nSave search session
Save all search sessions in the workspace\nSave workspace
Copy the report to the clipboard\nCopy
Save the report to a file\nSave Report
Modify the fields and layout of the report\nReport Options
Show or hide the menu bar\nToggle Menu Bar
Copy the list of errors to the clipboard\nCopy to Clipboard
Save the list of errors to a file\nSave Errors
Which folder would you like to select?
Unable to create XSL parser using specified Prog Id.
An error occurred while trying to open the file browser. Please make sure the file name is valid before trying to browse.
This will reset all Extension settings back to their original installation state. Are you sure?
Please specify a sound to play when a file is found
Please specify a sound to play when a search ends
Unable to play sound. The sound file may be corrupt or not in a valid waveform format
Please specify a valid sound file to play.
Unable to browse for sound file.
Program is configured for operation from a USB device. Shell settings cannot be set.
This will reset ALL configuration settings to the installation defaults. Are you sure?
Trial license has expired. You MUST register to continue legally using this product.
Fully functional time limited trial. Days remaining: %d
Command line search was not successful.
is not a recognized date/time format.
%s is not a valid time format
Unable to retrieve search criteria. Add failed.
Please open or create a search that you would like to add to favorites.
Trial period has expired. You need to either purchase a license or switch to the Free feature set.
The registration key has either been deleted or is unavailable. Please contact support@mythicsoft.com for more information.
Unable to load history configuration file
Failed to open file in Visual Studio. For more information please check the log file.
No External Editor specified. Would you like to specify one now?
Expecting a value for command line flag '-%s' but none provided
Command line flag '-%s' is not valid

** LOAD FAILED **

Unable to load: %s

Please check that the file still exists and that you have the appropriate security permissions to read it.

Please contact technical support if you need more assistance and include both this error text and any log files stored in the folder:
%s

More information:
%s
Errors occurred during search. If the problem persists please contact technical support
Unable to load criteria file. Please check that it is a valid criteria file.
You are about to overwrite a Favorite. Are you sure?
A new version of this program is available. Would you like to see the details?
Registered! Thank you for taking the time to register
Registration information is not valid. Please make sure ALL registration information is entered exactly as shown in the registration email.
Please specify a valid file name for saving the results.
Please specify a valid transform file.
Custom format file is not valid. Please check that the file exists and that you have appropriate security permissions to open the file.\r\n\r\n
Unable to open the file selection dialog: %d

Expression was found in text.
Expression start: %d
Expression length: %d
Found: %s
Installation log failed to start, please try installing again. If the problem persists please contact technical support.
\r\nThe directory name must be a valid path and include the drive letter (e.g. C:\Windows) OR a properly constructed UNC name (e.g. \\WORKSTATION\Shared_Folder).
(Recipient list could not be loaded - Content tech support for more information)\r\n
Error reported but no details provided.
Item name validation script failed with:
Containing text Active Scripting is not supported with Boolean searches which are matching the expression across the whole file. Switch to 'Line By Line' in the Options tab.
Script file does not exist or cannot be opened.
%s is not a valid path on this machine.
Invalid use of NEAR operator. No preceding operand found.
One or more of the selected files are composite files and will not be deleted. Do you want to continue?
Delete did not complete successfully. Some of the files were not deleted.
Warning: Operation failed on %d of the files processed. See log for more details.
Unable to access all items for drag drop. Operation cancelled.
Warning: Operation (%s) failed on %d of the selected files. See log for more details.
Unable to load editor. If the problem persists please contact technical support
Do you want to also remove all the user data associated with the program?
Are you sure you want to delete the favourite '%s'?
Are you sure you want to delete the search filter '%s'?
Insufficient memory to start new search. Please close previous searches and/or other applications and try again.

Because regular expressions work slightly differently than Wildcard expressions the result of the Expression Wizard may look different to what you are used to. e.g. The Wildcard expression 'filename.*' as a regular expression is '^filename\.*'.

Searches are performed on a line by line basis through the file looking for all lines that match the defined expression. The expression allows you to define how the line should begin, followed by what it should contain, and then how it should end.
This is the most recent version of the program.
An error occurred while trying to check for updates, please check log for more details.
%s already exists.\r\n\r\nDo you want to replace it?
Opening parenthesis '(' not found for corresponding closing parenthesis ')'. Use quotes to specify literal text, e.g. "ending)"

Closing parenthesis ')' not found for corresponding opening parenthesis '('. Use quotes to specify literal text, e.g. "(starting" or "Method("

Error position: %d
Text: %s
The keyword '%s' could not be compiled. Please check documentation for correct use of the keyword.
Changing the cache location will delete all currently cached text. Do you want to continue?
Unable to delete cache database. Please check you have no other instances of the application open and try again.
The Windows Shell could not create a context menu for the selected files. Would you like to continue with a restricted context menu?
The %s report is only valid when searching file contents
Save failed. Please make sure that you have the appropriate permissions to write to the specified file and folder.
Apply a filter to the search results
No files/folders found that match search criteria
'%s' is not valid for the expression type
An error has occurred. If the problem persists please contact technical support.
Folders Only limits the search to just folders, i.e. no files will be returned.
Files Only limits the search to just files, i.e. no folders will be returned.
Documents limits the search to only files with standard document extensions.
Music limits the search to only files with standard audio file extensions.
Pictures limits the search to only files with standard image file extensions.
Video limits the search to only files with standard video file extensions.
Error - Search could not be started
Copy the found items to another location or zip file.
Save the current search session, or complete workspace, to a file which can then be used to re-load the search results into %APP_NAME%.
Export the search results to an external format, such as Text, CSV, HTML, XML etc.
Search criteria excluded %s items from list
Search subfolders has been switched off
Containing text field consists of only whitespace characters
File Name match case is ON
Containing Text match case is ON
Floating License: up to %s concurrent users
Not copied - files already exist in destination
No index selected. Please pick an index and try again.
Synchronizing folder name with index name
Indexed: %s | Name only: %s | Errors: %s | Invalid: %s | Removed: %s | Unchanged: %s
Are you sure you want to delete: %s?
Index size: %s | Indexed count: %s | Name only: %s | Total: %s
Indexes are currently being updated. Do you want to cancel all indexing processes?
Index is in Read Only mode.
Primary search method failed, raw data search used.
Search term looks like a File List expression but the configuration option is switched off.
File name looks like a File List expression but the configuration option is switched off.
Search expression was evaluated on a Line by Line basis, as specified in Options tab.
Commas used in Wildcard expression, was semi-colon intended?
Update Required - Index does not match settings
Index does not support case sensitive searches. To enable this option you will need to rebuild the index with case sensitive searching enabled.
Index is out of date, the search has been run using old index settings.
Unable to display hits for file.
Index is empty, use the Index Manager to build the index.
Invalid date. A system file time cannot be prior to 1601.
File was only partially searched due to the number of found lines exceeding specified maximum.
Processing FILELIST operator generated an error
Do not show this message again
The selected file is very large and opening it might cause the program to slow down or stop responding.
For this feature to work the program will need to enable 3rd party browser extensions in Internet Explorer and you will need to reboot before it takes effect. \r\n\r\nWould you like to continue?
No content search threads currently running for this search
No locations to index have been specified. Please specify which locations should be indexed.
No content has been specified for indexing. Please specify which files should be included in the index.
Index list is locked for updates by another application
The location chosen to store the index database must be a new or empty folder.

A problem has occurred with the current operation. This is normally due to the setting 'Individual taskbar tab previews' in the User Interface Settings. If this continues to be a problem please turn this option OFF.
No Index Name has been specified.
No Index Path has been specified.
If you change the criteria type during a search the current search will be cancelled. Do you want to cancel the current search?
Path was excluded from searching due to active exclusion paths and/or filters
Empty file, no searchable text found
Unable to separate expression into individual terms
Expression type was changed to Boolean to support multiple search terms.
Do you want to permanently delete the selected items?
One or more very large lines of text were split into shorter lines, which might have an affect on the search hits.
Rename failed. Please check that the file was not locked.
Add reference to an existing index
Index path and name do not match
Keyword %s is only available in the Professional version. If you are simply searching for the term "%s" then please enclose it in quotes.
Preparing program for first time use...
This feature is not available in the free version of the program.
Reduced functionality version, some features will be hidden or disabled. Free for both personal and commercial use.
Fully functional version for trial period.

NOT REGISTERED! 
This product is only licensed for personal use at home. 

Commercial Users:

You MUST register this product to continue using it in a commercial environment otherwise you are violating the terms and conditions of the user agreement.

For a FREE registration code please go to:
https://www.mythicsoft.com/agentransack/register

Not registered. 
Commercial users of this product are required to register once the trial period has finished.

For a FREE registration code please go to:
https://www.mythicsoft.com/agentransack/register
Please specify how you use the product.
Changes will take effect when you restart the product.
The registration code entered is for the Lite version of the product so some features will now be disabled.
The free version of the program does not support the feature: %s
%APP_NAME% requires initial setup. Please run the main %APP_NAME% application to complete setup and then try again.
Warnings/Errors reported see Summary for more information (Ctrl+0)
Unable to create new index. Please check that you have write access to:
Invalid composite file, using standard search instead.
Anchor used in Wildcard expression when matching full path instead of just file name.
Index was created with an older version of the product and does not support search fields.
Would you like to save the indexing progress or revert back to the previous index state?
All shared Pro licenses are currently being used, would you like to wait or continue in Lite mode.
All Pro licenses are currently in use would you like to Wait for a license or Close the program?
Would you like to Wait or Close the program?
Unable to change the license folder. Please check the folder exists and that you have read/write permissions for the folder.
Folder has subfolders but no indexed items
Errors/Warnings reported, see Update Log for more information.
Do you want to close all search results?
Number must be between the range: %d to %d
Please specify at least one day for the schedule
Delete schedule and stop any scheduled updates or real-time monitoring:
Index was created in a previous version of %APP_NAME%. Please update the index to view index details.
Please select an update schedule for the index
Do you want permanently delete the index or just remove it from the list?
Floating license configuration settings could not be loaded.
No floating licenses found in folder. Continue?
This feature has been disabled by your system administrator.
The File Name field contains a wildcard character but expression type is set to Plain Text.
Would you like to generate and send an error report?
Before you can perform an index search you need to create an index. Would you like to create an index?
Please select the index you would like to search.
The latest update did not complete successfully.
Index was created using a newer version of %APP_NAME%, please upgrade this installation to view index details. Updating the index will recreate and restore the index to this version of %APP_NAME%.
Primary search method failed, using backup method.
Would you like to use registration information stored in clipboard?
